description: Complete the graphify-driven knowledge flywheel so Raw and Output both compile into revisioned ontology assets, graph reasoning can persist across chat turns via memory bridges, and graph query feedback loops back into the system safely.

userStories.title: Replace graphify compile stub with a real compiler runner
userStories.description: As the knowledge compiler, I want desktop graph builds to call a real graphify pipeline runner instead of assuming an unsupported `graphify <path>` CLI entry so that graph.json, GRAPH_REPORT.md, and graph.html are produced reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop graph compilation no longer assumes `graphify <path>` as a valid package CLI entrypoint
A real runner produces graphify assets for a corpus by invoking supported graphify module capabilities or a repo-local runner script
Graph compile returns graph.json, GRAPH_REPORT.md, and graph.html paths plus stdout/stderr when graphify succeeds
Fallback compiler remains available when graphify is missing or runner execution fails
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Persist ontology revisions and latest pointers
userStories.description: As the ontology store, I want every compile to create a revisioned graph asset record so that chat, memory, and UI can tell which graph version they are using.
acceptanceCriteria: Ontology persistence records revision_id, compiled_at, compiler_backend, and source_signature for each imported graph
The store exposes a latest pointer per ontology identity without deleting prior revisions
UI-facing ontology reads can resolve both latest revision and explicit revision lookups
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Import graphify assets by revision into ontology and output stores
userStories.description: As the importer, I want graph.json, GRAPH_REPORT.md, and graph.html to map into revision-aware OntologyDocument and OutputArtifact records so that graphify outputs become first-class iClaw objects.
acceptanceCriteria: graph.json import maps into a revision-aware OntologyDocument with graphify metadata paths
GRAPH_REPORT.md import upserts a revision-aware OutputArtifact with stable dedupe_key and artifact refs
graph.html path is attached to the same revision metadata and is available to the ontology view
Import paths do not duplicate output records across repeated compiles of the same revision
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Record revision-aware graph compiler jobs
userStories.description: As a user, I want graph compile jobs to expose queued/running/succeeded/fallback/failed state with revision details so that the flywheel feels observable instead of silent.
acceptanceCriteria: Graph compiler jobs persist status, backend, source ids, ontology revision ids, and error details
Jobs transition through running to succeeded, fallback, or failed for raw_ingest and output_feedback triggers
Ontology status panel shows latest compiler job state and linked revision identifiers
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Auto-run graphify update after save-result memory writes
userStories.description: As the feedback loop, I want graph query memory written by `graphify save-result` to trigger an automatic graphify update so that the next ontology revision reflects what the user just explored.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful graphify save-result writes trigger a follow-up graphify update for the same corpus
The update path produces a new ontology revision instead of mutating the previous revision in place
Updated graphify report and html assets are re-imported after the save-result update completes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Persist graph reasoning session state for ontology exploration
userStories.description: As the knowledge-library chat surface, I want focused node, active path, and recent graph query results to persist as graph reasoning session state so that users can keep exploring without restarting from scratch each turn.
acceptanceCriteria: Ontology exploration stores focused node id, selected edge id, active path target, and latest graph query result in a revision-aware session state object
Reloading or switching between graph and page view preserves the current graph reasoning state for the active ontology revision
Embedded chat automation reads this session state when deciding whether to run automatic graph queries
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Write graph context cards into OpenClaw memory after graph interactions
userStories.description: As the chat system, I want completed graph queries and shortest-path explorations to emit compact graph context cards into OpenClaw memory so that later turns can reuse graph reasoning without copying the whole graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful graph query and shortest-path actions write graph_context cards containing ontology_id, revision_id, focused node ids, query summary, and source output ids
Memory cards store only compressed graph context and never copy the full graph.json payload
Graph context write paths can be disabled or skipped cleanly when memory is unavailable
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Inject valid graph context memory into chat with revision checks
userStories.description: As a user, I want embedded and main chat to reuse stored graph context only when it matches the current ontology revision so that graph-guided answers stay relevant and stale memory does not drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat injection reads graph_context cards and checks ontology revision before using them
Revision mismatches downgrade stale graph context to non-authoritative hints or skip it entirely
Embedded auto-graph-query and main-chat graph prompt injection both honor the same revision validation logic
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Expose node and edge evidence as dedicated detail panels
userStories.description: As a user, I want dedicated node and edge evidence panels with raw/chunk/source metadata so that graph navigation explains why a node or edge exists instead of only showing summaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting a node shows a dedicated evidence panel with raw ids, chunk ids, excerpts, and graphify source metadata
Selecting an edge shows a dedicated evidence panel with relation type, source/target nodes, evidence links, and source_file/source_location
Evidence panels support navigating from a node or edge to neighboring nodes without losing the current selection state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Promote graph query and path results to first-class output artifacts
userStories.description: As the flywheel, I want graph query notes and path explanations to be storable as explicit output artifacts so that high-value graph reasoning can be reused, exported, and recompiled like other成果.
acceptanceCriteria: Graph query and shortest-path actions can save a first-class output artifact with stable dedupe_key and lineage metadata
Saved graph reasoning outputs appear under Output / 成果 and remain linked to the originating ontology revision
Saving a graph reasoning output triggers the normal output_feedback compiler path
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
